monsters.english_name: The Shark-Spirit of the Deep Route
monsters.province_origin: Cebu, Bohol, Leyte, Surigao, Sulu coastal waters
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Maritime Philippine folklore and Spanish-era sea monster terminology
monsters.passive_ability: Blood in the Current — Gains speed and critical chance against Bleeding, Marked, or low-HP enemies while in Sea or Rain conditions.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Red Current Pursuit — Locks onto a wounded enemy through the water, dealing Sea/Blood damage and applying Marked.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Shark-Spirit Spiral — Circles the battlefield in a violent current, damaging all enemies and applying Soaked.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Bite, Water Strike, Mark Prey, Blood Drain, Haste, Evasive Step, Sea Roar, Soaked, Quick Slash, Ambush, Fear Cry, Guard Break, Tide Step, Counter Ready, Tsunami
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires ending wasteful hunting and restoring a safe sea route
monsters.habitat: Open Sea, Coral Reef, Storm Coast, Fishing Village
monsters.weather_preference: Rain, Heavy Rain, Sea Breeze, Typhoon
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Clean fish, red shell charm, repaired net, sea salt, oath of sustainable fishing
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Tiburones is a name whispered by sailors for shark-like spirits that haunt deep routes between islands. In ALAMAT, a Tiburones is not merely an animal but a guardian-predator of the sea road, appearing when blood, greed, and fear disturb the water. This Tiburones begins attacking boats after fishers start dumping poisoned bait to force large catches. Villagers call it a monster, but the old navigators know it only follows the scent of imbalance. To earn its trust, the player must expose the poisoned bait trade, return injured sea creatures to the reef, and guide a boat through the old route without spilling blood. Once bonded, Tiburones becomes a fast hunter that excels at tracking wounded enemies, marking prey, and striking through currents.
monsters.folklore_summary: Philippine maritime folklore includes many sea monsters, giant fish, and dangerous water beings. The name Tiburones draws from Spanish-influenced sea vocabulary and is adapted here as a shark-spirit archetype for ALAMAT.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Tiburones as a sea-route predator guardian that responds to imbalance in fishing, travel, and bloodshed on the water.
monsters.physical_description: A large spectral shark-beast with indigo-gray hide, glowing red current markings, fin edges like obsidian blades, ghostly water trailing from its gills, and eyes like lanterns beneath the sea.
monsters.weaknesses: Lightning, Drought, poisoned bait, broken sea-oaths, loud metal gongs underwater
monsters.resistances: Sea, Blood, Soaked, Fear, speed reduction in water
monsters.npc_related: Ka Ambo the fisherman; Luningning the reef diver; Datu Baybay the coastal elder
monsters.boss_variant: Tiburones ng Pulang Agos — circles the battlefield and executes wounded targets.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Lason-Tiburones — poisoned shark spirit that spreads Rot through bites.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Malinis na Ruta — cleansed sea-route guardian with silver-blue markings.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A low underwater growl followed by a rushing current and snapping teeth.
monsters.drop_items: Shark-Spirit Tooth, Red Current Scale, Deep Route Fin, Salted Blood Pearl
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Shell Charm, Relic Weapon, Tattoo Mark, Anting-Anting, Beadwork
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Tiburones, a Filipino folklore-inspired shark spirit: spectral indigo-gray shark-beast, red current markings, obsidian fin edges, glowing sea-lantern eyes, water trails from gills, dynamic swimming pose, transparent background, readable chibi RPG monster silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Tiburones, shark-spirit sea guardian, indigo-gray hide, red current markings, glowing eyes under dark water, stormy island route background, fierce but protective RPG creature art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Tiburones vanishes into a spiral of red current, circles the target faster and faster, then erupts upward in a shark-shaped wave that marks enemies with glowing water scars.
monsters.design_notes: Use as a maritime predator guardian, not a generic shark. Tie mechanics to blood, routes, and sustainable fishing.

monsters.province_origin: Ilocos, Pangasinan, Cagayan Valley forest communities
monsters.passive_ability: Tiny Footstep Trick — Has increased evasion against large enemies and gains bonus Luck in Forest or Mist conditions.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Borrowed Voice Prank — Mimics an ally or enemy voice, applying Confusion and lowering accuracy.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Leaf-Button Snare — Sets a tiny forest trap that Roots one enemy and grants Hidden to Kibaan.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Confuse, Evasive Step, Haste, Smoke Veil, Root Prison, Leaf Storm, Trick Path, Blind Dust, Taunt, Mark Prey, Quick Claw, Spirit Ward, Cleanse, Barrier, Calm
monsters.capture_difficulty: Medium / Trust Difficulty: Requires winning a harmless prank exchange and returning stolen forest buttons
monsters.habitat: Bamboo Thicket, Balete Grove, Rice Terraces, Highland Village, Moonlit Clearing
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Rice cakes, tiny cloth buttons, forest berries, coconut sugar, carved toy
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Kibaan are small forest spirits remembered in northern stories as mischievous beings that confuse travelers, steal small objects, and delight in harmless pranks. In ALAMAT, Kibaan live in the seams of ordinary life: between roots, under woven baskets, behind house posts, and along footpaths where people forget to say thank you. This Kibaan has stolen buttons, beads, and small tools from a village not out of malice but because villagers cut through a sacred grove without leaving offerings. To earn its trust, the player must play by its rules: follow tiny footprints, return each stolen trinket to the correct owner, and leave a gift small enough for the Kibaan to carry. Once bonded, Kibaan becomes a fast trickster focused on evasion, confusion, traps, and utility rather than raw damage.
monsters.folklore_summary: Kibaan are small spirit beings in northern Philippine folklore, often associated with forests, mischief, and tricks on humans. Details vary by locality and oral tradition.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT frames Kibaan as tiny boundary spirits who punish carelessness with pranks and reward respect with guidance through hidden paths.
monsters.physical_description: A small child-sized forest spirit with leaf-like ears, dark curious eyes, twig-thin limbs, woven grass vest, button necklaces, mossy hair, and glowing footprints that appear only when it wants to be followed.
monsters.weaknesses: Honest laughter, bell chimes, direct sunlight in open fields, anti-illusion skills
monsters.resistances: Trickster, Forest, Confusion, Root, evasion penalties
monsters.npc_related: Tala the forest child; Mang Doro the grove cutter; Apo Raniag the trail elder
monsters.boss_variant: Kibaan ng Isang Daang Butones — creates many decoy traps and fake voices.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Kibaan ng Nawalang Tawa — pranks become harmful and cause Despair.
monsters.divine_variant: Munting Gabay ng Gubat — a bright guide-spirit that reveals hidden forest routes.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A tiny giggle moving from left to right, followed by rustling leaves and clicking buttons.
monsters.drop_items: Forest Button, Tiny Footprint Dust, Moss Thread, Trickster Berry
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Spirit Mask, Bell, Woven Garb, Beadwork, Herbal Bundle
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Kibaan inspired by northern Philippine folklore: tiny forest trickster spirit, leaf-like ears, mossy hair, button necklace, woven grass vest, playful stance, glowing tiny footprints, transparent background, readable chibi RPG style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Kibaan, small Filipino forest trickster spirit, leaf ears, dark curious eyes, moss hair, button ornaments, bamboo and balete grove background, playful magical RPG art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Kibaan giggles and vanishes behind leaves, enemy hears a copied voice, tiny glowing buttons scatter into a trap, roots snap upward as Kibaan appears Hidden.
monsters.design_notes: Small utility creature. Keep pranks mostly playful unless corrupted.

monsters.province_origin: Samar, Leyte, Bohol, Mindanao forest regions
monsters.passive_ability: Canopy Bruiser — Gains attack after using movement skills and takes reduced damage from Forest hazards.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Canopy Crash — Leaps from above and slams into one enemy, dealing Beast/Forest damage and reducing defense.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Wild Drum Chestbeat — Beats its chest like a forest drum, granting Frenzy to itself and Haste to Beast allies.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Heavy Strike, Leap, Guard Break, Roar, Haste, Earth Slam, Leaf Storm, Taunt, Counter Ready, Protect Ally, Stomp, Quick Claw, Fortify, Nature Shield, Reckless Charge
monsters.capture_difficulty: Medium / Trust Difficulty: Requires defending its canopy from hunters and returning a drum log
monsters.habitat: Balete Grove, Bamboo Thicket, Mountain Peak, Corrupted Wilds
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Banana bunch, wild honey, root crops, clean water, carved drum log
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Ungo are remembered in various stories as wild forest beings—part animal, part spirit, and part warning against entering deep woods without respect. In ALAMAT, Ungo are canopy-dwelling forest humanoids who communicate through chestbeats, branch drums, and low calls. This Ungo begins attacking hunters who leave iron traps in the canopy and cut drum logs from sacred trees. The village fears it as a savage beast, but the player learns it is protecting young forest animals caught in the traps. To earn trust, the player must remove the traps, return the stolen drum log, and respond to its rhythm without drawing a weapon. Once bonded, Ungo becomes a mobile warrior that leaps between positions, breaks defenses, and empowers other Beast-affiliated allies.
monsters.folklore_summary: Ungo is used in some Philippine folk contexts for frightening beings or wild creatures, with regional variation in meaning. ALAMAT adapts it as a forest ape-spirit archetype inspired by wild humanoid forest tales.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Ungo as a canopy guardian whose apparent savagery is a protective response to disrespectful hunting and forest destruction.
monsters.physical_description: A powerful ape-like forest spirit with brown-black fur, moss patches, amber eyes, long arms, bark bracelets, vine belt, and drum-like chest markings that glow when it beats a rhythm.
monsters.weaknesses: Fire, metal traps, silence effects, open-field exposure, poison darts
monsters.resistances: Beast, Forest, Earth, knockback, fear, root hazards
monsters.npc_related: Tala the forest child; Mang Doro the trapper; Apo Luntian the canopy elder
monsters.boss_variant: Ungo Punong-Kanopi — calls branch echoes and gains attack after every leap.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Ungo ng Putol na Gubat — enraged by logging, spreads Frenzy to all beasts.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Kanopi — golden-moss canopy guardian that drums healing rhythms.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A deep ape-like call followed by rhythmic chestbeats echoing through trees.
monsters.drop_items: Canopy Fur, Drum Bark, Amber Eye Seed, Vine Wristband
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Totem, Relic Weapon, Woven Garb, Herbal Bundle, Tattoo Mark
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Ungo, a Filipino folklore-inspired wild forest ape spirit: powerful ape-like humanoid, brown-black fur, moss patches, amber eyes, long arms, bark bracelets, vine belt, glowing drum chest markings, transparent background, readable chibi RPG warrior silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Ungo, wild Filipino forest ape spirit, mossy fur, amber eyes, bark bracelets, glowing chest drum markings, dense canopy background, fierce protective RPG creature art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Ungo swings from above, beats its glowing chest like a drum, leaps down in a canopy crash, and sends a shockwave of leaves and broken branches across the battlefield.
monsters.design_notes: Use as mobile physical fighter. Avoid making it generic gorilla; emphasize rhythm, canopy, and forest guardianship.

monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Tagalog and regional horse-trickster folklore variant
monsters.passive_ability: Bristle of Misdirection — When hit by a single-target attack, has a chance to lower the attacker's accuracy. Stronger in Fog or Mist.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Bristle-Reversal Curse — Fires enchanted mane bristles that reverse enemy buffs into minor debuffs.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Misty Wrong Turn — Covers the battlefield in forest mist, applying Blind and changing enemy target priority.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Confuse, Blind Dust, Trick Path, Smoke Veil, Haste, Wind Dash, Taunt, Evasive Step, Root Prison, Fear Cry, Weaken, Mark Prey, Counter Ready, Calm, Spirit Ward
monsters.capture_difficulty: Medium-Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires recovering a stolen mane bristle without trying to dominate it
monsters.habitat: Bamboo Thicket, Balete Grove, Mountain Peak, Moonlit Clearing
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Wild grass, coconut sugar, brass bell, mountain salt, woven bridle left untied
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Tigbalang in ALAMAT is treated as a regional cousin or variant of the better-known Tikbalang, but it occupies a different role in the Codex. Where the Tikbalang guards crossroads and grand mountain thresholds, Tigbalang haunts smaller forest paths, misty shortcuts, and trails where arrogant travelers try to force their way through. Its mane bristles are said to confuse direction, and some hunters believe stealing one grants control over the creature. This Tigbalang appears after a bristle is stolen and used to mislead villagers into a logging claim. To earn trust, the player must retrieve the bristle, refuse to use it as a leash, and walk the mist path without demanding mastery. Once bonded, Tigbalang becomes a controller that reverses buffs, blinds enemies, and turns mist into a tactical weapon.
monsters.folklore_summary: Tigbalang is treated here as a regional spelling or variant connected to Tikbalang/Tigbalang horse-like trickster traditions in Philippine folklore. ALAMAT separates it mechanically as a mist-path variant rather than duplicating Tikbalang's exact role.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT uses Tigbalang as a smaller, bristled forest-mist trickster focused on misdirection, reversed trails, and resisting domination through stolen mane magic.
monsters.physical_description: A lean horse-headed forest spirit smaller than Tikbalang, with bristled dark mane, moss-streaked limbs, mist-wrapped hooves, long arms, amber-green eyes, and loose grass cords hanging from its wrists.
monsters.weaknesses: Truth-name rituals, Sun, calm effects, returned-bristle rites, anti-mist skills
monsters.resistances: Trickster, Mist, Blind, Confusion, speed reduction, path manipulation
monsters.npc_related: Berto the trail hunter; Mayang the guide; Apo Luntian the forest elder
monsters.boss_variant: Tigbalang ng Ulap-Gubat — reverses all buffs during mist phases.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Suwail na Tigbalang — uses stolen bristles to trap travelers permanently.
monsters.divine_variant: Gabay ng Malabong Landas — mist-path guide that protects humble travelers.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A short horse snort muffled by fog, followed by rustling mane bristles.
monsters.drop_items: Mist Mane Bristle, Reversed Hoofprint, Grass Cord, Fog Bell
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Bell, Spirit Mask, Woven Garb, Beadwork, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Tigbalang, a Filipino folklore-inspired bristled horse-headed forest trickster: lean horse-headed humanoid, dark bristled mane, mossy limbs, mist-wrapped hooves, amber-green eyes, loose grass cords, transparent background, readable chibi RPG controller silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Tigbalang, bristled forest-mist horse trickster from Philippine folklore, smaller and mistier than Tikbalang, amber-green eyes, dark mane bristles, foggy bamboo path background, sly RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Tigbalang shakes its bristled mane, enchanted hairs scatter like needles, enemy buffs flip into smoky debuffs, then mist rolls over the field and turns targets around.
monsters.design_notes: Because Tikbalang already exists, make Tigbalang a regional mist-path variant with distinct mechanics and smaller silhouette.

monsters.province_origin: Bicol, Visayas, and Mindanao forest stories
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Regional Philippine folklore with varied spellings and descriptions
monsters.passive_ability: Shame-Laugh Hex — Enemies that miss Tambaluslos have a chance to become Confused or Weakened.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Backward Clothing Curse — Forces enemies into magical embarrassment, lowering attack, accuracy, and speed.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Long-Limbed Snatch — Extends distorted limbs to steal one enemy buff and deal Shadow/Trickster damage.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Confuse, Taunt, Blind Dust, Smoke Veil, Shadow Step, Weaken, Haste, Curse, Trick Path, Evasive Step, Fear Cry, Mark Prey, Silence, Ambush, Calm
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires surviving its humiliation tricks without anger and restoring a broken forest taboo
monsters.habitat: Bamboo Thicket, Balete Grove, Corrupted Wilds, Market Town outskirts, Spirit Threshold
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Coconut wine, banana leaf bundle, joke offering, forest fruit, untied sash
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Tambaluslos is a grotesque and mischievous forest being in Philippine folklore, often associated with deception, humiliation, and strange body proportions. In ALAMAT, Tambaluslos is a taboo-enforcer that uses embarrassment rather than direct violence. It appears when humans enter sacred forest places with arrogance, greed, or mockery. This Tambaluslos has been causing travelers to stumble back into town with clothes reversed, tools missing, and stories too embarrassing to tell. The player discovers that the tricks began after a group of merchants laughed at a forest offering site and turned it into a gambling stop. To earn trust, the player must endure the Tambaluslos' tricks without anger, restore the taboo marker, and make the creature laugh honestly rather than cruelly. Once bonded, Tambaluslos becomes a disruptive trickster that steals buffs, causes confusion, and punishes pride.
monsters.folklore_summary: Tambaluslos is a Philippine folklore being often described as a strange, long-limbed or grotesque trickster associated with confusing, frightening, or humiliating travelers. Descriptions vary by region.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT interprets Tambaluslos as a forest taboo trickster whose humiliating pranks are meant to humble arrogance and protect disrespected places.
monsters.physical_description: A tall thin humanoid with exaggerated long arms and legs, wide mischievous grin, messy hair, backward-worn forest cloth, elongated fingers, and a shadow that laughs separately from its body. Design should be uncanny but not obscene.
monsters.weaknesses: Humility, honest laughter, calm effects, Sacred taboo restoration, anti-illusion skills
monsters.resistances: Trickster, Shadow, Confusion, Taunt, Blind, evasion penalties
monsters.npc_related: Mang Pilo the gambler; Tala the forest child; Apo Sikat the taboo-marker keeper
monsters.boss_variant: Tambaluslos ng Baliktad na Daan — reverses controls, buffs, and battlefield positions.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Halimaw ng Mapangutyang Tawa — turns jokes into Despair and fear loops.
monsters.divine_variant: Tagapagpatawa ng Mapagmataas — cleansed trickster who humbles tyrants without cruelty.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A high mocking laugh that bounces between trees, followed by snapping cloth and finger taps.
monsters.drop_items: Backward Sash, Laughing Shadow Thread, Trickster Finger Bone, Forest Taboo Token
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Spirit Mask, Woven Garb, Bell, Anting-Anting, Tattoo Mark
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Tambaluslos inspired by Philippine folklore: tall thin long-limbed forest trickster, exaggerated arms and legs, mischievous grin, backward-worn forest cloth, laughing shadow, uncanny but not obscene, transparent background, readable chibi RPG trickster silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Tambaluslos, Filipino long-limbed forest trickster, wide mocking grin, messy hair, backward cloth, elongated fingers, shadow laughing separately, foggy forest background, eerie humorous RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Tambaluslos laughs, flips its sash backward, enemy clothing and buffs invert as glyphs spin, then long arms stretch unnaturally to snatch a glowing buff from the target.
monsters.design_notes: Avoid explicit or obscene visual details sometimes found in crude retellings. Focus on long-limbed trickery, embarrassment, and taboo enforcement.
